title: ChatMessage
description: Lobby chat envelope. Authored by docs/architecture/chat-safety.md. Carried over a dedicated WebRTC `chat` DataChannel (id=2) — never on the deterministic command channel. Receive-side validators MUST run NFKC normalization, control/bidi strip, and length cap (240) BEFORE schema check, and MUST rewrite `senderId` to the transport peer's canonical id at ingress. The optional `sig` field is reserved for the long-lived peer-key signature contract introduced by the TLS / WebRTC authentication plan.

v.description: Envelope version. Bumped only on a breaking shape change.

scope.description: Where the message originates. `ingame` is reserved for a future in-game chat (currently out of scope per docs/architecture/chat-safety.md § Scope).

senderId.description: Stable peer identifier in the `peer:<id>` shape. Rewritten by the receiver to the transport peer's canonical id; see chat-safety.md § Envelope.

t.description: Milliseconds since session start on the sending peer. Presentation-only; deterministic gameplay never reads this field.

nonce.description: Per-message nonce. Used for client-side deduplication and dropping duplicate envelopes; never reused inside the same session.

text.description: Plain-text body, NFKC-normalized, control/bidi-stripped, single-line. Length is measured in UTF-16 code units to match runtime String.length.

sig.description: Reserved. Optional in v1. Becomes a mandatory signature over the canonical-JSON envelope under the long-lived peer key once the TLS / WebRTC authentication plan lands.
